title: Restore CAP_NET_RAW
body:
Add CAP_NET_RAW back to the default list of granted capabilities.

Add capabilities lists to `inspect` output.

comments.body:
Interested in how/why this came about.
Did something break that required this capability?
How is the default list of kernel caps determined?
Me too.  I know that it is needed for ping, but not sure of anything else.  I want to remove it from CRI-O by default also.
The actual changes LGTM, but am interested in the conversation started by others.
Yeah, I realized that when dropping it from #799, I broke ping.

Except for CAP_NET_RAW, the default list in #799 is the default list that we were previously getting from the generator in [runtime-tools](https://github.com/opencontainers/runtime-tools/blob/master/generate/generate.go#L77), which is also the list dockerd uses (confirmed with 'docker run --rm -it fedora capsh --print').

What do you think of dropping the capability and setting the syscall to allow ping.
There is a network namespaces sysctl that allows ping to send pings without net_raw
net.ipv4.ping_group_range

Once we allow customization of sysctl in crio I want to ship this on by default for the root user, but no NET_RAW.
BTW I have heard there have been a couple of NET_RAW CVE's that allows containers to see other network trafic then is defined in their network namespace.

This won't help unprivileged users, who currently won't get CAP_NET_RAW abilities on the host's network namespace, and privileged users are served equally well by #818, so dropping this one in favor of that.
